---
title: "Additional R Resources"
description: "Supplementary training materials and community resources for R users at OUCRU."
toc: false
number-sections: false
format: 
  html:
    link-external-newwindow: true
    link-external-icon: true
date: last-modified
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY -- HH:mm:ss (z)"
---

Welcome! Beyond this medical statistics course, we highly recommend exploring these dedicated R programming resources developed by the OUCRU Math Modelling and Biostatistics groups.

---

## 📚 Training & Community

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [R Training 2025](https://oucru-modelling.github.io/R-training-2025/)
::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Foundational R Skills** A comprehensive course covering the essentials of R, from data manipulation with `dplyr` to professional visualization with `ggplot2`. Perfect for those who want a solid programming foundation before diving deeper into statistics.
[Go to Course →](https://oucru-modelling.github.io/R-training-2025/){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm role="button"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [R Cafe Materials](https://oucru-modelling.github.io/R-cafe-materials/)
::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
**Continuous Learning** The "R Cafe" is our community-driven space for sharing tips, advanced tricks, and solving specific coding challenges. These materials include slide decks and scripts from our regular meetups.
[Browse Materials →](https://oucru-modelling.github.io/R-cafe-materials/){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm role="button"}
:::
:::

:::

---

### How to use these resources
* **New to R?** Start with the **R Training 2025** link to get comfortable with the syntax.
* **Need specific solutions?** Search the **R Cafe Materials** for localized examples and community-tested code.

If you have questions about any of these materials, feel free to leave a comment below or reach out to the Biostatistics group.
